description: Design a distributed rate limiting system for a multi-tenant API gateway. Requirements: (1) per-tenant rate limits configurable independently, (2) limit enforcement within 100ms of a burst being detected across multiple gateway nodes, (3) graceful degradation when the rate-limit coordination service is unavailable — gateway must not become a single point of failure, (4) audit log of every rate limit decision written before the request is allowed or rejected.
context: Current state: simple in-memory per-node rate limiting with no cross-node coordination. Problem: a burst of 1000 req/s from tenant A hits node 1 while tenant A's quota is 200 req/s globally — other nodes don't know and allow the full quota each, so effective limit is 200 × N nodes. Coordination latency budget: 100ms. Graceful degradation: if coordination unavailable, fall back to per-node limits (accept more traffic rather than drop all). Audit requirement: write the decision record to an append-only log before sending the HTTP response.

checks.text: Does the proposal describe cross-node coordination for rate limit enforcement within 100ms?
Does the proposal provide graceful degradation when the coordination service is unavailable (fallback to per-node limits rather than complete failure)?
Does the proposal write the audit log entry before allowing or rejecting the request?
